name: Basic White Sauce with Milk (Bechamel)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan combine the milk, chopped onion, grated carrot, nutmeg, and white pepper.
2. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
3. Cover, remove from heat, and allow to steep for ten minutes.
4. Pour the warm milk through a strainer, pressing down on the vegetables to obtain all the juices. Let cool.
5. In a bowl combine the instant nonfat dry milk, cornstarch or arrowroot, and flour.
6. Gradually add one or two tablespoons of the cooled milk to the dry mixture, stirring until a smooth paste forms.
7. Return the milk to the saucepan. Add the flour paste and stir over medium heat until the mixture boils and thickens.
8. If the sauce will not be used immediately, cover and refrigerate. Cold sauce will thicken; thin with a little flavored cooking juice, wine, or stock as desired.
9. Makes about 1 cup; recipe is easily doubled.
Vegetables steeped in hot milk add flavor to this versatile sauce, which can replace the butter-flour-milk white sauce in any of your recipes. It also forms the base of a salt-free cheese sauce that in its turn can dress vegetables, open-faced sandwiches, macaroni or baked potatoes.
